title: High-Protein Mango Froyo
tagline: Frozen mango blended with Greek yogurt and mango protein into a soft-serve you don't need a machine for.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. Genuinely good macros for a dessert — high protein, low fat, and sweetened with stevia instead of sugar.
The protein comes from the Greek yogurt (~200-250 g) plus 1 scoop mango protein powder; no need for soya here.
Freezing the mango and using thick yogurt (not extra milk) keeps it from turning icy or chalky. Don't add more than 1 scoop of protein powder or the texture goes stretchy.
Creamier / higher-cal treat version: add 1 tbsp cream, 1 tbsp milk powder, half a banana or 8-10 soaked cashews. Cutting version: more ice, more yogurt, less milk, lean on stevia.

items: 1 large ripe mango (~200 g), cubed and frozen 4-5 hours
2-4 stevia tablets, dissolved in a spoon of warm milk/water first
Few drops lemon juice (makes the mango pop)
Tiny splash vanilla or pinch cardamom
Toppings: roasted almonds, crushed digestive biscuit, dark chocolate shavings, saffron, toasted coconut

steps.title: Freeze the mango
steps.body: Cube 1 large mango (~200 g) and freeze at least 4-5 hours. Freezing the fruit is the trick that gives body and stops it going icy — don't skip it.

steps.title: Dissolve the stevia
steps.body: Dissolve 2-4 stevia tablets in a spoon of warm milk or water first, so it blends in evenly instead of leaving a bitter speck.

steps.title: Blend thick
steps.body: Blend the frozen mango + 200-250 g Greek yogurt + 1 scoop mango protein powder + the dissolved stevia + 1 pinch salt. Add a few drops of lemon if using. Keep the yogurt thick and go easy on liquid.

steps.title: Adjust to soft-serve
steps.body: Add the 60-100 ml milk slowly while blending, only as much as needed to reach a thick soft-serve consistency. Too much milk makes it thin and icy.

steps.title: Eat now or firm up
steps.body: Eat immediately as froyo, or for more of an ice-cream texture freeze another 1-2 hours — stir once at ~45 min so it doesn't set icy. Top with roasted almonds, dark chocolate shavings or crushed biscuit.

notes: Fresh mango + mango protein together tastes far better than protein powder alone — the real fruit fixes the artificial flavour.
Protein powders can turn frozen desserts chalky or stretchy: 1 scoop max, thick yogurt, a pinch of salt, and not too much milk keeps it smooth.
If you happen to have xanthan gum, ~1/4 tsp is the secret behind 'anabolic ice cream' texture — it stops iciness in a low-fat mix.
Fastest lazy version: just blend frozen mango + 1 scoop protein + ice + milk for something between a smoothie bowl and soft-serve.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
